title: Vibrant Venezuelan Collards Stew
description: A pungent, cheese-pulled stew that blends collards with a rich soffritto base, perfect for meal-prep and Ayurvedic-Rajasic friendly. It delivers hearty flavor with a creamy touch.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion, garlic, and diced red bell pepper, sautéing until the vegetables soften and become fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and red pepper flakes, cooking for another minute to develop depth.
Add the chopped collard greens, tossing to coat them in the sauce, cooking until they wilt slightly, about 3 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, and let it cook for 15 minutes so the flavors meld.
Stir in the drained chickpeas, cooking for an additional 5 minutes to heat through.
Taste and season with salt and black pepper, adjusting to preference.
Remove the pot from heat and sprinkle crumbled queso fresco evenly over the stew.
Cover the pot, allow the cheese to melt into the hot liquid for 2 minutes, then stir to combine.
Serve hot, or divide into meal-prep containers for up to 4 days in the refrigerator.
